review_execution.command: claude -p --model claude-opus-4-8 --effort max --permission-mode dontAsk --tools "" --output-format json < staged diff prompt > /tmp/cp00-104-claude-review-output-valid.json

parsed_review.rationale: CP00-104 binds the Risk C failure taxonomy and evidence catalog over range RP01.P06.M09.S07-RP01.P07.M08.S17 (unit_count 150) and is internally consistent across all changed files. Coverage math is sound: 8 P06 review-extension units (M09.S07-S11, M10.S01-S03) plus the P07 failure-scenario units sum to exactly 150 (CP104_FAILURE_MICRO_PHASE_COUNTS = 3+11+11+20*5+17 = 142 scenarios; 8+142=150), unit IDs are unique (P06 extension IDs disjoint from P07 scenario IDs), and the validator gates length===150, uniqueness, the six required anchor units (incl. M09.S07, M09.S11, M10.S03, P07.M00.S01, P07.M03.S20, P07.M08.S17), 8 review extensions, 20 taxonomy categories, 142 scenarios, manifest 150, and fixture/test/smoke counts 6/6/5 (which match the category-index derivation: failure_fixture cat15 in 6 phases, failure_unit_test cat16 in 6 phases, failure_integration_smoke cat17 in 5 phases since M08 count 17<18). P06 review-extension bindings (ai_retrieval review_required, audit_hint_fields display-only, matched_rule_capture, deny_over_allow blocked, legal_hold blocked, permission_matrix_row, view allow, search allow-with-trimming) are reference/decision bindings only. Every item produced by cp104FreezeItem is frozen and carries synthetic_only:true, uses_real_client_data:false, and false for evaluates_runtime_permission, writes_audit_event, writes_product_state, creates_database_rows, executes_ai_retrieval, executes_export_download, executes_external_share, mutates_locks, retries_operations, performs_rollback, performs_compensation, and ldip_implemented; the validator re-asserts all of these across reviewExtensions, taxonomy, and scenarios. The failure/audit/permission/recovery policies are explicitly expectation/reference-only (audit_hint_only_until_rp03_audit_kernel, expected_denial_only_until_rp02_permission_kernel, rollback_compensation_retry_expectations_only), and the audit_event_id appears only as an expected hint field, not an audit write. Boundary verification holds for synthetic-only, reference-only, no runtime permission evaluation, no audit ledger writes, no product state writes, no database rows, no real client data, no AI retrieval/export/external-share execution, no lock mutation, no retry/rollback/compensation execution, and no LDIP implementation. The contract JSON failure_taxonomy_contract is cross-checked field-by-field by validate-rp01-core-domain-contract.mjs against the JS contract (pack_id, contract_id, source range, upstream CP00-103 contract id, all no-write booleans, covered_unit_count 150, all 11 covered micro phases, all 8 review extensions, all 20 categories, all 13 forbidden claims, and four policies), CP00-103 is correctly demoted to permission_review_packet_pack while current_pack becomes CP00-104 with production_ready_flag core_domain_failure_taxonomy_evidence_catalog_verified, and the schema_version bump v0.10->v0.11 is reflected. production_ready gating is adequate: the node:test suite asserts binding/contract/extension/taxonomy/scenario/fixture/evidence/handoff/coverage invariants and coverage.valid===true, the .mjs validator runs validateCoreDomainCp104Coverage and asserts valid plus all counts and next_subphase_id RP01.P07.M08.S18, and the Hermes evidence packet (gate H01) anchors node --test, rp01:core-domain:validate, closeout-pack:validate CP00-104, and build. The Claude review packet correctly declares model claude-opus-4-8, effort max, read_only true, and exactly_one_valid_pack_review_required. No boundary violations, no gating gaps, and no destructive or runtime behavior were found in the staged diff.
notes: No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings were reported by the valid CP00-104 pack-level Claude review.
